exampleSentences: Anh ấy làm việc chăm chỉ, hơn nữa còn rất thân thiện với mọi người.
Chị đã hoàn thành bài tập, hơn nữa còn giúp em học toán nữa.
Em thích món ăn này, hơn nữa nó cũng rất tốt cho sức khỏe.
notes:
hơn nữa = moreover, furthermore. It adds an extra point that strengthens what you just said.

It’s a linking phrase, used to add more information with a slightly stronger or “on top of that” feeling. Often comes after a comma in the middle of a sentence or at the start of the second clause.

Stronger and more formal than và (and). Similar to thêm vào đó, but hơn nữa feels a bit more emphatic.
notes_md:
**hơn nữa** = *moreover*, *furthermore*. It adds an extra point that strengthens what you just said.

It’s a linking phrase, used to add more information with a slightly stronger or “on top of that” feeling. Often comes after a comma in the middle of a sentence or at the start of the second clause.


Stronger and more formal than **và** (and). Similar to **thêm vào đó**, but **hơn nữa** feels a bit more emphatic.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He works hard, moreover he is very friendly to everyone.
She finished her homework, moreover she helped me with math too.
I like this dish, moreover it is also very good for your health.
